pick.rationale: Purdue enters as an overwhelming favorite in this 2/15 seed matchup, ranking #8 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with the nation's #1 offense that should exploit Queens' 316th-ranked defense. The efficiency gap is staggering, with Purdue's +30.5 adjusted efficiency margin dwarfing Queens' -1.45, reflecting a massive talent and competition level disparity between the Big Ten powerhouse and first-time NCAA Tournament participant. While Queens earned their bid through an Atlantic Sun Tournament championship and will have the emotional boost of their program's historic debut, the 25.5-point betting spread and historical 2-seed success rate make Purdue the clear choice in this Round of 64 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Purdue vs Queens - 2/15 Seed Mismatch Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Purdue's elite #1 ranked offense and top-10 overall profile create an insurmountable advantage over first-time tournament participant Queens, whose 316th-ranked defense cannot contain the Boilermakers' firepower.
reasoningStep.evidence: Purdue ranks #8 in KenPom/Torvik with 26-8 record, #1 offense (132.6 adjOE), and +30.5 efficiency margin. Queens ranks 181st-199th with 21-13 record, 316th defense, and -1.45 efficiency margin. Betting line has Purdue as 25.5-point favorite. Historical 2-seed vs 15-seed win rate exceeds 80%.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

They will make their first ever appearance in the NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament in 2026 . The team, currently led by ...

modelTrace.content:
I have enough information to make a confident prediction. Let me summarize what I know:

Purdue:
- Ranked #8 in both KenPom and Torvik
- 26-8 record
- #1 offense in Torvik (132.6 adjOE), #2 in KenPom (131 adjO)
- Solid defense (#36 in Torvik, ~100.5 in KenPom)
- Big Ten Tournament champion
- 2-seed in West region

Queens:
- Ranked 181st (KenPom) / 199th (Torvik)
- 21-13 record
- 78th-85th offense
- 316th defense in Torvik (117.3 in KenPom)
- First-ever NCAA Tournament appearance in program history
- Atlantic Sun Conference Tournament champion
- 15-seed in West region

The efficiency gap is massive - roughly 32 points in adjusted efficiency margin. Purdue has the #1 offense in the nation facing Queens' 316th-ranked defense. This is a classic 2/15 mismatch.

Historical context: 2-seeds beat 15-seeds approximately 80%+ of the time in NCAA Tournament history. While there have been famous upsets (like UMBC over Virginia in 2018, or more recently), the vast majority of 2-seeds advance.

Queens is making their first-ever NCAA Tournament appearance, which is a historic moment but also means they lack tournament experience. Purdue is a Big Ten powerhouse with tournament experience.

The betting line has Purdue as a 25.5-point favorite, which aligns with the efficiency metrics.

I don't see any major injury concerns for Purdue that would significantly impact this game. The earlier search mentioned some vague "upset alert" but that seems to be about Purdue's tournament run overall, not this specific game.

This should be a high-confidence pick for Purdue. Looking at my prior picks, I gave UConn 88% confidence in their 2/15 matchup against Furman, and Arizona 94% in their 1/16 matchup. Purdue vs Queens is similar to UConn vs Furman - both are 2-seeds with massive efficiency advantages. Purdue's offense is even more elite than UConn's typically is (#1 vs top-25), so I'll go with similar high confidence.

Let me finalize my pick.

  "pick": {

    "confidence": 90,
    "rationale": "Purdue enters as an overwhelming favorite in this 2/15 seed matchup, ranking #8 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with the nation's #1 offense (132.6 adjusted efficiency) that should exploit Queens' 316th-ranked defense. The efficiency gap is staggering, with Purdue's +30.5 adjusted efficiency margin dwarfing Queens' -1.45, reflecting a massive talent and competition level disparity between the Big Ten powerhouse and first-time NCAA Tournament participant. While Queens earned their bid through an Atlantic Sun Tournament championship and will have the emotional boost of their program's historic debut, the 25.5-point betting spread and historical 2-seed success rate (over 80%) make Purdue the clear choice in this Round of 64 contest."
  },

    "evidence": "Purdue ranks #8 in KenPom/Torvik with 26-8 record, #1 offense (132.6 adjOE), and +30.5 efficiency margin. Queens ranks 181st-199th with 21-13 record, 316th defense, and -1.45 efficiency margin. Betting line has Purdue as 25.5-point favorite. Historical 2-seed vs 15-seed win rate exceeds 80%."
  }
}
